description_long: This clock tower features a vertical column adorned with numerous round clock faces, each encased in a gold or brass-like bezel, giving the structure a clustered, almost organic appearance. The clocks are arranged irregularly along the column, creating a visually dynamic and sculptural effect. The base of the tower is a solid, rectangular plinth with subtle relief patterns, likely made of stone or concrete, providing stability and contrast to the ornate upper section. The clock faces are white with dark hands and markings, and the bezels are metallic, contributing to a sense of opulence and complexity. There is no visible roof or spire; the top of the column continues the clustered clock motif. The overall design is modern and artistic, standing out as a public art installation as much as a functional timepiece.
description: A tall, sculptural clock tower with numerous gold-framed round clock faces clustered along a vertical column atop a solid rectangular base. The design is modern and artistic, with white clock faces and metallic accents.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile of the column with several clock faces visible, and the rectangular base with relief patterns.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the dense arrangement of clock faces along the column and the full width of the rectangular base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with a clear look at the vertical alignment of the clocks and the base's texture.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back cluster of clock faces and the rectangular base, with less detail on the clock faces themselves.
attribute_clock_face_design: The clock faces are round with white backgrounds, dark hour and minute hands, and simple black tick markings for hours and minutes. The bezels are metallic gold, and there are no numerals or decorative elements beyond the clustered arrangement.
